2606.13285.title: Once-for-All: Scalable Simultaneous Forecasting via Equilibrium State Estimation
2606.13285.paper_summary: We introduce Equilibrium State Estimation (ESE), a novel paradigm for simultaneous prediction, where multiple interacting systems require separate yet coordinated forecasts. Such scenarios often arise in real-world settings such as economics and healthcare modeling. Unlike existing approaches that predict one system at a time, ESE forecasts all systems in a single pass. It first estimates the equilibrium state across systems, then generates holistic forecasts based on the difference between the current state and the estimated equilibrium. Extensive experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets, including currency exchange and COVID-19 spread modeling, demonstrate that ESE is at least as accurate as state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods while being significantly faster. In addition, ESE integrates seamlessly with conventional predictors, combining their accuracy with its exceptional efficiency and delivering a 10-70x speedup. With linear-time complexity, ESE scales far better than SOTA methods as the number of systems increases. Moreover, it remains accurate under diverse perturbations, establishing ESE as a fast, generalizable, robust, and scalable multi-prediction method.
2606.13285.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹了平衡狀態估計（Equilibrium State Estimation，ESE），這是一種新穎的同時預測範式，其中多個互動系統需要分開但協調的預測。這種情況在現實世界中經常出現，例如經濟學和醫療保健建模。與現有的逐一預測系統的方法不同，ESE在一次過程中預測所有系統。它首先估計系統之間的平衡狀態，然後根據當前狀態與估計平衡之間的差異生成整體預測。在合成和真實世界數據集上的大量實驗，包括貨幣兌換和COVID-19傳播建模，證明ESE至少與最先進的方法（SOTA）一樣準確，同時速度顯著更快。此外，ESE與傳統預測器無縫集成，將它們的準確性與其卓越的效率結合，實現了10-70倍的加速。由於其線性時間複雜度，隨著系統數量的增加，ESE的擴展性遠超過SOTA方法。此外，它在各種擾動下仍然保持準確性，確立了ESE作為一種快速、可泛化、穩健且可擴展的多重預測方法。
2606.13285.author: Beinan Xu et.al.
2606.13285.authors: Beinan Xu, Andy Song, Jiti Gao, Feng Liu
